name: moon_build_wave_d_moon_ui
outcome_goal: Build Moon Build v1 — an orbital build surface with a single adaptive dock, event-driven state, and ring state system. Replaces the current useless builder.

jobs.label: moon_presenter
moon_reducer
moon_glyphs_css
jobs.prompt:
TASK: Create the Moon Build glyph icon set and CSS.

WHAT TO DO:

1. Create `Code&DBs/Workflow/surfaces/app/src/moon/MoonGlyph.tsx`:
   - A React component that renders custom inline SVG glyphs based on a `type` prop.
   - Props: { type: GlyphType, size?: number, color?: string }
   - GlyphType: 'step' | 'gate' | 'state' | 'trigger' | 'human' | 'binding'
   - Each glyph is a simple, recognizable SVG icon:
     - step: circle with play arrow
     - gate: shield with checkmark
     - state: cylinder (database)
     - trigger: lightning bolt
     - human: person silhouette
     - binding: chain link
   - SVGs should be 24x24 viewBox, stroke-based, no fills (outline style).
   - No icon library. Pure inline SVG paths.

2. Create `Code&DBs/Workflow/surfaces/app/src/moon/moon-build.css`:
   - CSS custom properties for the Moon Build theme:
     --moon-bg: #0d1117
     --moon-surface: #161b22
     --moon-border: #30363d
     --moon-ring-grounded: #e6edf3 (white)
     --moon-ring-selected: #58a6ff (blue)
     --moon-ring-projected: #8b949e dashed
     --moon-ring-blocked: #f85149 (red/amber)
     --moon-dock-width: 33vw
     --moon-orbit-radius: 280px
   - .moon-page: full viewport, dark bg, flex column
   - .moon-orbit: center area, relative positioning for nodes
   - .moon-node: absolute positioned, ring border, contains glyph
   - .moon-node--grounded/selected/projected/blocked: ring color variants
   - .moon-edge: SVG path between nodes
   - .moon-dock: slide-in panel from the right, 33vw width, transitions
   - .moon-dock--open: transform translateX(0)
   - .moon-release: bottom tray, slides up
   - .moon-stepper: vertical step flow inside dock
   - Transitions: transform 200ms ease, opacity 150ms ease. No animation library.

RULES:
- No icon library. No external CSS. No animation library.
- CSS variables only — no hardcoded colors in rules.
- Dark theme only (matches existing app theme from tokens.css).
- All SVG paths hand-written, minimal, recognizable at 24px.

OUTPUT: New: moon/MoonGlyph.tsx, moon/moon-build.css

jobs.label: moon_orbit_surface
jobs.prompt:
TASK: Build the center orbit component — the main visualization of the Moon Build UI.

CONTEXT:
- moonBuildPresenter.ts provides presentBuild() which returns MoonBuildViewModel with positioned OrbitNodes and OrbitEdges.
- MoonGlyph.tsx renders SVG icons by type.
- moon-build.css has all the CSS classes.

WHAT TO DO:

Create `Code&DBs/Workflow/surfaces/app/src/moon/MoonOrbitSurface.tsx`:

Props: { viewModel: MoonBuildViewModel, selectedNodeId: string | null, onSelectNode: (id: string | null) => void, onOpenDock: (dock: string) => void }

Renders:
1. SVG container for edges — paths connecting nodes along the orbit. Dominant path edges get a brighter stroke.
2. Node layer — absolutely positioned divs for each OrbitNode:
   - Circle border with ring state color (from CSS class .moon-node--{ringState})
   - MoonGlyph inside with the node's glyphType
   - Title label below (only for selected node and its neighbors)
   - Badge indicators (branch/parallel/loop) as small overlays
3. Click handlers: clicking a node calls onSelectNode(node.id). Clicking empty space calls onSelectNode(null).
4. The orbit itself is an SVG ellipse path (decorative, low opacity) showing the orbital track.

RULES:
- No state management — this is a pure render component. State comes from props.
- Use CSS classes from moon-build.css, don't inline styles.
- Nodes are positioned via absolute positioning using the position.x/y from the view model.
- Keep it simple. No drag, no zoom, no animation beyond CSS transitions.

OUTPUT: New: moon/MoonOrbitSurface.tsx

jobs.label: moon_dock
jobs.prompt:
TASK: Build the dock system — a single adaptive right-side panel with tabs for Action/Context/Connect, plus the stepper.

CONTEXT:
- moonBuildReducer manages: openDock, dockTab, stepperStage, stepperPath.
- MoonBuildViewModel.dockContent has: actionOptions, contextAttachments, connectBindings.
- Dock slides in from the right, 33vw wide, pushes orbit inward.

WHAT TO DO:

1. Create `Code&DBs/Workflow/surfaces/app/src/moon/MoonDockHost.tsx`:
   - Props: { isOpen: boolean, activeTab: string | null, content: DockContent | null, node: OrbitNode | null, onClose: () => void, onTabChange: (tab: string) => void, onMutate: (subpath: string, body: any) => Promise<void> }
   - Renders: slide-in panel with 3 tabs at top (Action, Context, Connect)
   - Each tab renders its content from DockContent
   - Uses CSS class .moon-dock / .moon-dock--open

2. Create `Code&DBs/Workflow/surfaces/app/src/moon/MoonDockStepper.tsx`:
   - Props: { tab: string, node: OrbitNode, content: DockContent, stage: number, onNext: () => void, onBack: () => void, onMutate: (subpath: string, body: any) => Promise<void> }
   - For Action tab: step 1 = choose action archetype (from actionOptions), step 2 = configure details, step 3 = confirm
   - For Context tab: step 1 = choose context type, step 2 = select/upload, step 3 = confirm attachment
   - For Connect tab: step 1 = choose system, step 2 = choose operation, step 3 = confirm binding
   - Assist-first: if only one option at a step, auto-advance. Show prefilled defaults.
   - Back button always available. 'Advanced' link at bottom to skip to raw config.

3. Create `Code&DBs/Workflow/surfaces/app/src/moon/MoonReleaseTray.tsx`:
   - Props: { isOpen: boolean, release: ReleaseStatus, onToggle: () => void, onPublish: () => void }
   - Bottom tray that slides up. Shows: readiness badge, blocker list, projected jobs/triggers, publish button.
   - Independent of side dock (can coexist).

RULES:
- Use CSS classes from moon-build.css.
- Dock and release are controlled components — state comes from reducer via props.
- onMutate calls the shared controller's postBuildMutation. The dock doesn't know about API endpoints.
- Keep stepper simple for v1. Each step is a list of options or a form. No fancy animations.

OUTPUT: New: moon/MoonDockHost.tsx, moon/MoonDockStepper.tsx, moon/MoonReleaseTray.tsx

jobs.label: moon_build_page
moon_shell_integration
jobs.prompt:
TASK: Integrate Moon Build into the app shell as the new build route.

CONTEXT:
- App.tsx contains the shell with tab bar and route rendering.
- shell/state.ts defines ShellState with buildView: 'workspace' | 'builder'.
- The current build tab renders either BuildWorkspace or BuilderPage based on buildView.
- MoonBuildPage is the new component.

WHAT TO DO:

1. In `Code&DBs/Workflow/surfaces/app/src/shell/state.ts`:
   - Add 'moon' to the BuildView type: `type BuildView = 'workspace' | 'builder' | 'moon'`
   - Set the default buildView to 'moon' (so Moon Build is the default)

2. In `Code&DBs/Workflow/surfaces/app/src/App.tsx`:
   - Add lazy import: `const MoonBuildPage = React.lazy(() => import('./moon/MoonBuildPage').then(m => ({ default: m.MoonBuildPage })))`
   - In the build tab render logic, add: `if (buildView === 'moon') return <MoonBuildPage workflowId={buildWorkflowId} />`
   - Add a small toggle in the build tab header (icon or dropdown) to switch between 'moon', 'workspace', 'builder' views. This lets users fall back to the old views.

3. URL handling: add `?page=moon` as a recognized route that sets buildView='moon'.

RULES:
- Old views (workspace, builder) must still work. This is additive.
- Moon is the NEW DEFAULT. Users can switch back.
- Keep the toggle minimal — a small dropdown or segmented control, not a major UI element.

OUTPUT: Modified: App.tsx, shell/state.ts

jobs.label: moon_review
jobs.prompt:
TASK: Review all Moon Build code for correctness, type safety, and architectural consistency.

CHECK:
1. Type safety: Run `npx tsc --noEmit` in surfaces/app. Report all errors.
2. Presenter purity: moonBuildPresenter.ts must have NO imports from React, no fetch, no DOM access.
3. Reducer completeness: every MoonBuildAction type must be handled.
4. CSS isolation: moon-build.css must not affect any classes outside .moon-* namespace.
5. Event-driven state: MoonBuildPage must use useBuildPayload (SSE), not manual fetch/setInterval.
6. No circular dependencies between moon/ modules.
7. Shell integration: old views still renderable, Moon is default.
8. Shared controller: BuilderPage and BuildWorkspace still import from shared/ correctly.

For each issue found, describe the file, line, and fix needed. If no issues, say so.

OUTPUT: Review report written to artifacts/workflow/moon-build-waves/wave_d_output/review_report.md
